imp: verb. To engraft or plant (a plant or part of one, a sapling, etc.).
impi: noun. A group of Zulu (or other Bantu) warriors; a detachment of armed men.
imprint: noun. An impression; the mark left behind by printing something or by pressing on something, or the figurative counterpart of such a mark.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
iris: noun. A plant of the genus Iris, common in the northern hemisphere, and generally having attractive blooms (See Iris (plant) on Wikipedia.
ism: noun. An ideology, system of thought, or practice that can be described by a word ending in -ism.
mint: noun. A building or institution where money (originally, only coins) is produced under government licence.
mir: noun. A traditional village community in Imperial Russia, characterised by self-government and collectivist control of local lands.
mis: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for unencoded languages.
misprint: noun. An accidental mistake in print.
mist: noun. Water or other liquid finely suspended in air.
mitis: noun. A process for producing malleable iron castings by melting wrought iron, to which from 0.05 to 0.1 per cent of aluminum is added to
nims: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of nim
nip: verb. To catch and enclose or compress tightly between two surfaces, or points which are brought together or closed; to pinch; to close in
nit: noun. The egg of a louse.
pin: noun. A needle without an eye (usually) made of drawn-out steel wire with one end sharpened and the other flattened or rounded into a head,
pint: noun. A unit of volume, equivalent to: one eighth of a gallon, specifically: 20 fluid ounces, approximately 568 millilitres (an imperial
pirn: noun. A cone-shaped spool or bobbin on which thread or yarn is wound, used most often for weaving.
pit: noun. A hole in the ground.
prim: adjective. Of a person, their manner or appearance: Formal and precise; stiffly decorous.
prims: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of prim
print: adjective. Of, relating to, or writing for printed publications.
prism: noun. A polyhedron with parallel ends of the same polygonal shape and size, the other faces being parallelogram - shaped sides.
psi: noun. The twenty-third letter of Classical and Modern Greek and the twenty-fifth letter of Old and Ancient Greek.
rim: noun. An edge around something, especially when circular.
rins: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rin
rip: verb. To divide or separate the parts of (especially something flimsy, such as paper or fabric), by cutting or tearing; to tear off or out
simp: noun. A simple person lacking common sense; a fool or simpleton.
sin: noun. A violation of divine will or religious law.
sip: noun. A small mouthful of drink.
sir: noun. A man of a higher rank or position.
sit: verb. To be in a position in which the upper body is upright and supported by the buttocks.
snip: verb. To cut with short sharp actions, as with scissors.
snit: noun. A temper; a bad mood.
spin: verb. To rotate, revolve, gyrate (usually quickly); to partially or completely rotate to face another direction.
spirit: noun. The soul of a person or other creature.
spit: noun. A thin metal or wooden rod on which meat is skewered for cooking, often over a fire.
sprint: noun. A short race at top speed.
sprit: noun. A spar between mast and upper outer corner of a spritsail on sailing boats.
sri: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siriano.
stir: verb. To disturb the relative position of the particles (of a liquid or similar) by passing an object through it.
stirp: noun. A line descended from a single ancestor.
strip: noun. A long, thin piece of land; any long, thin area.
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
tip: noun. The extreme end of something, especially when pointed; e.g. the sharp end of a pencil.
trim: verb. To reduce slightly; to cut; especially, to remove excess.
trip: noun. A journey; an excursion or jaunt.
imi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Anamgura.
isn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isanzu.
ist: noun. A practitioner or supporter of an ism.
mirin: noun. A form of Japanese rice wine, less alcoholic than saké and used in cooking.
mri: symbol. ISO 639-2 /T & ISO 639-3 language code for Māori.
mrs: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Maragus.
nis: noun. A nix; a hobgoblin, especially one that resides in a farm house.
pir: noun. A Muslim holy man or religious leader, especially Sufi.
pist: verb. Obsolete spelling of pissed (simple past and past participle of piss).
pitris: noun. The spirits of departed ancestors, venerated in Hindu culture.
pms: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Piedmontese.
ript: verb. simple past and past participle of rip
risp: verb. To rub together, to rasp or grate.
simi: noun. A short sword or dagger used by the Kikuyu people.
